oligos.name: Tn5ME-A (Nextera Read 1 transposon oligo)
oligos.role: Transposon adapter oligo carrying the Read 1 (s5) sequence plus the 19-bp mosaic end; loaded onto Tn5
oligos.notes: Standard ATAC-seq/Nextera transposon oligo used per the original ATAC-seq protocol (Buenrostro et al. 2013, ref 37); not printed verbatim in the paper.

oligos.name: Tn5ME-B (Nextera Read 2 transposon oligo)
oligos.role: Transposon adapter oligo carrying the Read 2 (s7) sequence plus the 19-bp mosaic end; loaded onto Tn5
oligos.notes: Standard ATAC-seq/Nextera transposon oligo (Buenrostro et al. 2013, ref 37).

oligos.name: Tn5MErev (mosaic-end reverse, 5'-phosphorylated)
oligos.role: Phosphorylated complementary mosaic-end strand annealed to Tn5ME-A and Tn5ME-B to form the double-stranded transposon end
oligos.notes: Carries a 5' phosphate (/5Phos/). Standard ATAC-seq transposon reverse oligo (ref 37).

oligos.name: Tn5 transposon A (ds, ME-A/ME-rev)
oligos.role: Assembled double-stranded transposon end (A side) loaded onto Tn5

oligos.name: Tn5 transposon B (ds, ME-B/ME-rev)
oligos.role: Assembled double-stranded transposon end (B side) loaded onto Tn5

oligos.name: Ad1 barcoding-PCR primer (P5 / i5-indexed)
oligos.role: Well-specific PCR primer adding P5, the i5 cell/sample index, and the Read 1 adapter; one of the 90 i5 barcodes

components.name: Nextera Read 1 (s5)+ME
components.role: anneals to the tagmented A end

oligos.notes: Per-cell identity comes from the combinatorial i5×i7 index (96 × 90 = 8640 combinations). Sequences follow the ATAC-seq barcoding primers of ref 37; barcode sequences not tabulated in the documents.

oligos.name: Ad2 barcoding-PCR primer (P7 / i7-indexed)
oligos.role: Well-specific PCR primer adding P7, the i7 cell/sample index, and the Read 2 adapter; one of the 96 i7 barcodes

components.name: Nextera Read 2 (s7)+ME
components.role: anneals to the tagmented B end

oligos.notes: Combinatorial with Ad1; 96 × 90 adapter combinations index 8640 single cells per experiment.

oligos.name: Nextera Read 1 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Read 1 sequencing primer; anneals to the s5/ME region and reads into the accessible-DNA insert

oligos.name: Nextera Read 2 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer; anneals to the s7/ME region and reads into the insert from the P7 side

oligos.name: Nextera i7 index sequencing primer
oligos.role: Index 1 (i7) read primer; reads the i7 cell/sample index

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: P5 flow-cell grafting sequence (5' end of the library top strand)

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: P7 flow-cell grafting sequence (3' end of the library top strand appears as its reverse complement)

final_library.source_label: Pi-ATAC final Illumina library (dual-indexed Nextera/ATAC-seq structure)
annotation_lines: [I5_INDEX:8] = i5 sample index (encodes the sorted well / cell)
[I7_INDEX:8] = i7 sample index (encodes the sorted well / cell)

evidence.locator: Pi-ATAC final Illumina library (dual-indexed Nextera/ATAC-seq structure)

library_generation.title: Fixation, permeabilization & antibody staining
library_generation.summary: Cells or nuclei are PFA-fixed, permeabilized, and stained with antibodies against surface/intracellular protein epitopes.
library_generation.note: 1% formaldehyde fixation (quenched with glycine) preserves protein epitopes and chromatin; permeabilization with mild detergent (e.g. NP-40/Igepal) allows intracellular and intranuclear antibody staining (e.g. EpCAM, CD45, HIF1a, GATA2, phospho-NF-kB). Chromatin still carries no adapters at this point.
library_generation.product:
5'- ==============  accessible / closed chromatin  ============== -3'
3'- ==============  accessible / closed chromatin  ============== -5'
         (fixed, permeabilized, antibody-stained; no adapters yet)

library_generation.title: Bulk Tn5 tagmentation of accessible chromatin
library_generation.summary: Tn5 loaded with Nextera mosaic-end adapters inserts Read 1/Read 2 adapters into accessible DNA and is stopped with EDTA (no purification).
library_generation.note: Hyperactive Tn5 carrying Tn5ME-A/ME-rev and Tn5ME-B/ME-rev cuts open chromatin and appends adapters, leaving a characteristic 9-bp gap on each strand. The reaction is quenched with 40 mM EDTA; no clean-up is performed so that the sample can be sorted directly.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG
                   3'- TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC/5Phos/
                                        [ accessible genomic DNA ]
                                                    /5Phos/CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGAGGGCGTCTCTG -3'
                                          GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG -5'
         (A-adapter on one end, B-adapter on the other; 9-bp gaps left by Tn5)

library_generation.title: Index-FACS single-cell sorting + reverse crosslinking
library_generation.summary: Single cells are index-sorted into wells prefilled with reverse-crosslink buffer, recording each cell's antibody fluorescence, then de-crosslinked.
library_generation.note: The FACS AriaII sorts one cell per well of a 96- or 384-well plate; the per-cell protein-epitope fluorescence intensities are stored (index sorting) and mapped to the well. Reverse crosslinking (65 °C overnight, proteinase K, then 80 °C inactivation) uses a Taq-compatible buffer so PCR can proceed directly on the same well.
library_generation.product:
per well (one cell):
  5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[ genomic DNA ]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT... -3'
  3'- ...           TCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[ genomic DNA ]GAGCTCGGGTGCTCTG... GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAG -5'
  (tagmented fragment + recorded protein index; 9-bp gaps to be filled by PCR polymerase)

library_generation.title: Well-specific combinatorial barcoding PCR
library_generation.summary: Each well is amplified with a unique i5 (Ad1) × i7 (Ad2) primer pair, gap-filling the tagmented ends and building the full P5/index/Read/P7 library.
library_generation.note: 72 °C extension first fills the 9-bp gaps; 20 cycles of PCR with the well's Ad1(i5)/Ad2(i7) primer combination add P5, the i5 index, the Read 1 adapter (5' side) and the Read 2 adapter, i7 index, P7 (3' side). The i5×i7 combination uniquely encodes the well/cell. Wells are pooled, size-selected (>150 bp), and purified.

library_sequencing.note: First end of the 75x2 paired-end run on Illumina HiSeq 4000. The Read 1 primer (identical to the s5+ME top-strand segment) anneals to the bottom strand and reads rightward into the accessible genomic-DNA insert.
Reads the 8-bp i7 index. Primer (ME-rc + s7-rc, identical to the top strand) anneals to the bottom strand and reads rightward across the i7; together with i5 it encodes the sorted well/cell.

On HiSeq 4000 the i5 index is read after paired-end resynthesis: the primer anneals just 3' of the i5 and reads leftward (toward P5) using the top strand as template. With i7 it encodes the sorted well/cell.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[i5]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAG...-3'
3'-TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG[i5]AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTC...-5'
                             <------- i5 index primer (top strand = template)
                                 (reads the 8-bp i5)

library_sequencing.note: Second end of the 75x2 paired-end run. The Read 2 primer anneals to the top strand at the s7+ME (ME-rc/s7-rc) region and reads leftward into the insert from the P7 side.

title: Pi-ATAC
description: Pi-ATAC (Protein-indexed Assay of Transposase Accessible Chromatin with sequencing) jointly profiles single-cell chromatin accessibility and protein-epitope abundance from the same cell. Cells or nuclei are fixed, permeabilized, antibody-stained, and tagmented in bulk with Tn5 loaded with Nextera mosaic-end adapters; single cells are then index-sorted by FACS into wells (recording per-cell antibody fluorescence) and reverse-crosslinked, and each well is amplified with a unique combinatorial i5×i7 barcoding-PCR primer pair that also builds the Illumina library. The paired-end reads capture accessible genomic DNA fragments, while the i5/i7 index combination encodes the well (and thus the cell) whose FACS protein levels are known.

original_publication.title: Joint single-cell DNA accessibility and protein epitope profiling reveals environmental regulation of epigenomic heterogeneity

throughput.summary: Sorting into 384-well plates with 96 × 90 adapter combinations indexes 8640 single cells in one experiment; ~1000 single cells sequenced per HiSeq 4000 lane at ~0.3 million reads per cell; cost ~$0.15-$1.50 per cell.
throughput.cells: Up to 8640 single cells per experiment (96 × 90 i7 × i5 adapter combinations)
throughput.dna: ~0.3 million reads per cell; single-cell libraries filtered at >=500 nuclear fragments

publication.statistical_model: Single-cell accessibility summarized as ChromVAR bias-corrected TF-motif deviations (accessibility deviation vs. GC/accessibility-matched background peak sets, normalized to a z-score); reproducibility/correlations assessed by Pearson correlation, and group comparisons by Wilcoxon and chi-squared tests.

other.label: Protocol
other.value: Step-by-step protocol deposited on protocols.io; library prep follows the original ATAC-seq protocol (Buenrostro et al. 2013)

data_processing.summary: Paired-end ATAC fragments are adapter/transposase-trimmed, aligned to hg19/mm9 with Bowtie2, deduplicated and mitochondria-filtered, then peak-called with MACS2; per-cell libraries passing fragment/FRiP filters are summarized as ChromVAR bias-corrected TF-motif accessibility deviations and integrated with the per-cell FACS protein-index values.
data_processing.statistical_model: ChromVAR bias-corrected TF motif-accessibility deviations (z-scores vs GC-/accessibility-matched background peak sets); Pearson correlation, Wilcoxon and chi-squared tests for group comparisons; DESeq2 normalization for bulk peak counts
